item.title: Terminal relocation demands more than policy decision. [ newagebd ]
item.description:
[Priority]

Systemic Significance

Selected by: Gemini-2.5-Flash-Lite, Llama-3.3-70B, Qwen-3-32B

THE intercity bus terminals in Dhaka have for long been regarded as a major contributor to the traffic congestion. Traffic congestion is estimated to cost Dhaka’s economy about Tk 37,000 crore annually while wasting around five million work hours each year. Rough estimates suggest that around 14,000 city buses and 8,000 inter-district buses operate in Dhaka, yet the city...

item.title: Increasing export competitiveness. [ today ]
item.description:
[Priority]

Systemic Significance

Selected by: Gemini-2.5-Flash-Lite, Llama-3.3-70B, Qwen-3-32B

The overall export earnings of Bangladesh for FY2025–26 stood at US$48 billion as of June 2026, according to Export Promotion Bureau (EPB) data, against a target of US$55 billion. Although exports fell well short of the target, export earnings in June increased compared with June 2025. Moreover, the knitwear and...

item.title: Cost of disparity in power distribution. [ today ]
item.description:
[Priority]

Systemic Significance

Selected by: Gemini-2.5-Flash-Lite, Llama-3.3-70B, Qwen-3-32B

Frequent load-shedding in rural areas is not only causing public suffering, but also badly affecting  economic activities. From hatcheries to poultry farms and from small cottage industries to healthcare facilities and other day-to-day operations are being hampered in every sector. Many villagers who have invested their lifesavings to set up...

item.title: Climate change loss in Bangladesh. [ today ]
item.description:
[Priority]

Systemic Significance

Selected by: Gemini-2.5-Flash-Lite, Llama-3.3-70B, Qwen-3-32B, GPT-OSS-120B, GPT-OSS-20

Bangladesh is widely considered one of the most climate vulnerable countries in the world. Its low lying deltaic geography, high population density, and heavy dependence on natural resources make it exceptionally exposed to climate related risks. Rising sea levels, erratic rainfall, floods, cyclones, and increasing temperatures are no longer future...
